memberdef.argsstring: (const Tensor &in_tensor, const bool &is_diag=false, const cytnx_int64 &rowrank=-1, const std::vector< std::string > &in_labels={}, const std::string &name="")
memberdef.name: UniTensor

briefdescription.para: Construct a UniTensor from a cytnx::Tensor.

parameterdescription.para: a cytnx::Tensor
whether the input Tensor in_tensor is a diagonal Tensor; this requires the input in_tensor to be 1D.
the rowrank of the resulting UniTensor

listitem.para: The constructed UniTensor will have the same rank as the input Tensor, with default labels, and a shared view (shared instance) of interal block as the input Tensor.
The constructed UniTensor is always untagged.

simplesect.para: The internal block of the UniTensor is a referece to the input Tensor. That is, they share the same memory. If the elements of the UniTensor are changed, the original Tensor is also changed and vice-versa. Use Tensor.clone() if a shared view is not desired.

memberdef.argsstring: (const Tensor &in_tensor, const bool &is_diag=false, const cytnx_int64 &rowrank=-1, const std::vector< std::string > &in_labels={}, const std::string &name="")
memberdef.name: Init

briefdescription.para: Initialize a UniTensor from a cytnx::Tensor.

parameterdescription.para: a cytnx::Tensor
whether the input Tensor in_tensor is a diagonal Tensor. this requires the input in_tensor to be 1D.
the rowrank of the resulting UniTensor.
user-specified name of the UniTensor.

listitem.para: The constructed UniTensor will have the same rank as the input Tensor, with default labels, and a shared view (shared instance) of interal block as the input Tensor.
The constructed UniTensor is always untagged.

simplesect.para: The internal block of the UniTensor is a referece to the input Tensor. That is, they share the same memory. If the elements of the UniTensor are changed, the original Tensor is also changed and vice-versa. Use Tensor.clone() if a shared view is not desired.
UniTensor(const Tensor &, const bool &, const cytnx_int64 &)

memberdef.name: UniTensor

briefdescription.para: Construct a UniTensor.

parameterdescription.para: the bond list. Each bond will be deep copy( not a shared view of bond object with input bond)
the labels for each rank(bond)
the rank of physical row space.
the data type of the UniTensor. It can be any type defined in cytnx::Type.
the device that the UniTensor is put on. It can be any device defined in cytnx::Device.
if the constructed UniTensor is a diagonal UniTensor. This can only be assigned true when the UniTensor is square and rank-2 UniTensor. The UniTensor must have one in-bond and one out-bond.

listitem.para: the bonds cannot contain simutaneously untagged bond(s) and tagged bond(s)
If the bonds are with symmetry (qnums), the symmetry types should be the same across all the bonds.

memberdef.argsstring: (const std::vector< Bond > &bonds, const std::vector< std::string > &in_labels={}, const cytnx_int64 &rowrank=-1, const unsigned int &dtype=Type.Double, const int &device=Device.cpu, const bool &is_diag=false, const std::string &name="")
memberdef.name: Init

briefdescription.para: Initialize the UniTensor with the given arguments.

detaileddescription.para:
This is the initial function of the UniTensor. If you want to initialize your UniTensor after declaring just a 'void' UniTensor. You can use this function to initialize it.
bonds
the bond list. Each bond will be deep copy( not a shared view of bond object with input bond)
in_labels
the labels for each rank(bond)
rowrank
the rank of physical row space
dtype
the dtype of the UniTensor. It can be any type defined in cytnx::Type.
device
the device that the UniTensor is put on. It can be any device defined in cytnx::Device.
is_diag
if the constructed UniTensor is a diagonal UniTensor. This can only be assigned true when the UniTensor is square, untagged and rank-2 UniTensor.
name
user specified name of the UniTensor.
Please ensure that all of the Bond in bonds should be all symmetric or non-symmetric. You cannot mix them.
UniTensor(const std::vector<Bond> &bonds, const std::vector<std::string> &in_labels, const cytnx_int64 &rowrank, const unsigned int &dtype, const int &device, const bool &is_diag)

briefdescription.para: permute the legs of the UniTensor without sign flips

detaileddescription.para:
The indices are permuted, but no fermionic sign flips occur. Use with care! This is usually not intended, since fermionic permutations create sign flips! This should typically only be used to compare tensors in different sign conventions with each other.
mapper
the mapper of the permutation by indices
rowrank
the new rowrank after the permutation
UniTensor
It is recommended to use permute_nosignflip(const std::vector<std::string> &mapper, const cytnx_int64 &rowrank) instead
Usually, the signs should change when permuting a fermionic UniTensor. Use permute instead, unless you are really sure you want to permute without signflips!

briefdescription.para: Apply a twist (or braids/self-swap) operation to a given bond; No effect for bosonic tensors; for a fermionic tensor, this means that a signflip occurs for all blocks where the bond has odd fermion parity.

parameterdescription.para: bond label on which the twist shall be applied

simplesect.para: This always applies the twist to the bond, ignoring its direction or whether they are incoming or outgoing bonds.

simplesect.para: This always applies the twist to the bond, ignoring its direction or whether they are incoming or outgoing bonds.

briefdescription.para: Inline version.

simplesect.para: twist(const cytnx_int64 &idx)

briefdescription.para: Apply twists to all right bonds (>= rowrank) with bond type BD_KET.

detaileddescription.para:
For bosonic tensors, nothing changes. For fermions, this makes sure that bra- and ket-states can be contracted correctly. For example, a scalar product <A|B> between ket states A and B represented by fermionic tensors with incoming and outgoing legs, can be calculated correctly by contract(Adag.fermion_twists_(), B). This example asumes that Adag is the dagger of A and has rowrank=0, while B has maximal rowrank. Applying fermion_twists_ to B would have no effect in this case and is thus safe to do. Similarly, this can be applied to linear operators. If M is an operator, then its first rowrank indices correspond to ket bonds and are not affected by fermion_twists_. For the remaining bonds, a twist is applied if they are not of type BD_BRA. The combination of this method to bra states, ket states and linear operators in a Hilbert space, together with contractions, ensures that scalar products can be executed as desired.
The rowrank must be set correctly before applying this method.

briefdescription.para: Inline version.

simplesect.para: The rowrank must be set correctly before applying this method.

briefdescription.para: Make the UniTensor contiguous by coalescing the memory (storage).

simplesect.para: If the input tensor is not contiguous, then the data will be stored in new memory; otherwise, the input tensor is returned and no cloning of the data happens.

briefdescription.para: Make the UniTensor contiguous by coalescing the memory (storage), inplacely.
Apply fermionic signflips to the UniTensor, such that all elements calling signflip() on the output tensor will result in all elements to be false.

simplesect.para: Blocks that need to be flipped are copied and inverted. Blocks where no signflip has to be applied remain. Therefore, these correspond to a shared view and changing these blocks on the output tensor will affect the elements of the input tensor as well.

briefdescription.para: Apply fermionic signflips to the UniTensor, inplacely. Subsequently, all elements returned by signflip() are false.
Plot the diagram of the UniTensor.

parameterdescription.para: whether need to print the information of the bonds of the UniTensor.

whether need to print the full information of the block

briefdescription.para: Group the same quantum number basis together.

detaileddescription.para:
If the UniTensor has duplicated quantum number, then this function will group the basis with the same quantum number.
The UniTensor must be in block form. That is, the UniTensor is UTenType::Block.

briefdescription.para: Get an element at a specific location.

parameterdescription.para: the location of the element we want to access.

simplesect.para: this API is only for C++.

For fermions, the signflip is not included and has to be multiplied by the user! The reason behind this is that several UniTensors in different permutations can share the same memory. Use signflip() to get the sign structure for each block.

labels and qidx forming one to one pairs. e.g. it means get qidx[i] qnum at Bond labels[i]. Also note that the return Tensor will have axes in the same order specified by labels.

For fermions, the signflip is not included and has to be multiplied by the user! The reason behind this is that several UniTensors in different permutations can share the same memory. Use signflip() to get the sign structure for each block.

briefdescription.para: Get all the blocks of the UniTensor.

detaileddescription.para:
get_blocks will return the blocks of the UniTensor. Furthermore,
For symmetric UniTensor, it will call contiguous() and then return the deep copy of blocks.

For non-symmetric UniTensor, it will return the deep copy of blocks.
std::vector<Tensor>
For fermions, the signflip is not included and has to be multiplied by the user! The reason behind this is that several UniTensors in different permutations can share the same memory. Use signflip() to get the sign structure for each block.

briefdescription.para: get elements using Accessor (C++ API) / slices (python API)

parameterdescription.para: the Accessor (C++ API) / slices (python API) to get the elements.

simplesect.para: [UniTensor]
Tensor::get, UniTensor::operator[]

listitem.para: The return will be a new UniTensor instance, which does not share memory with the current UniTensor.
Equivalently, one can also use the [] operator to access elements.
For diagonal UniTensors, the accessor list can have either one element (to address the diagonal elements), or two elements (in this case, the output will be a non-diagonal UniTensor).

briefdescription.para: get elements using Accessor (C++ API) / slices (python API)
set elements using Accessor (C++ API) / slices (python API)

parameterdescription.para: the Accessor (C++ API) / slices (python API) to set the elements.
the tensor containing the values to set.

simplesect.para: [UniTensor]
Tensor::set, UniTensor::operator[], UniTensor::get

listitem.para: The return will be a new UniTensor instance, which does not share memory with the current UniTensor.
Equivalently, one can also use the [] operator to access elements.

For diagonal UniTensors, the accessor list can have either one element (to address the diagonal elements; rhs must be one-dimensional), or two elements (in this case, the output will be a non-diagonal UniTensor; rhs must be two-dimensional).

simplesect.para: This function only works for non-symmetric UniTensor. That is, the UniTensor cannot be UTenType::Block.

briefdescription.para: Convert the UniTensor to non-diagonal form.

detaileddescription.para:
Converts a UniTensor with is_diag = true to one with is_diag = false by expanding each block into a (diagonal) matrix.
If the input tensor is already non-diagonal (is_diag = false), the input is returned. So every manipulation on the returned tensor will change the input tensor. Call clone() on the result if an independent copy is required.
UniTensor
to_dense_(), is_diag()

If force is true, it will combine the bonds anyway even the direction of the bonds are same. After combining, the direction of the bonds will be set as same as the first bond.

The UniTensor cannot be diagonal form (that means is_diag cannot be true.)

memberdef.name: contract

briefdescription.para: Contract the UniTensor with common labels.

detaileddescription.para:
This function contract the UniTensor legs with common labels.
inR
The UniTensor you want to contract with.
mv_elem_self
Whether the self UniTensor need to do contiguous() after the contraction.
mv_elem_rhs
Whether the rhs UniTensor inR need to do contiguous() after the contraction.
Two UniTensor need to have same UniTensor type, namely, same UTenType. You cannot contract symmetric to non-symmetric UniTensor.
You cannot contract tagged UniTensor and untagged UniTensor.
For Dense diagonal UniTensor, the type of Bond (bra-ket) should match.
For symmetric UniTensor (UTenType.Block), Symmetry, degeneracy, quantum numbers and Bond type should be consistent.
UniTensor
uten_type(), linalg::Tensordot

briefdescription.para: Return the norm of the UniTensor.

detaileddescription.para:
norm() return the 2-norm of the UniTensor $UT$ as a Scalar carrying the UniTensor's own precision (Float for Float/ComplexFloat input, Double otherwise), so x /= x.norm() stays dtype-preserving rather than promoting a Float UniTensor to Double. \[ ||UT||_2 \]
Scalar

briefdescription.para: Element-wise (pseudo-)inverse.

detaileddescription.para:
This function performs an element-wise inverse with clip. If $ |T_\text{in}[i]| \le \mathrm{clip} $, then the new element is set to zero. \[ T_\text{out}[i] = \left\{ \begin{array}{ll} 1/(T_\text{in}[i]) & \mathrm{if} \ |T_\text{in}[i]| > \mathrm{clip} \\ 0 & \mathrm{otherwise} \end{array} \right. \]
clip
elmements with absolute value <= clip are set to zero, corresponding to the pseudo-inverse; default: -1 (no clipping)
UniTensor
For complex type UniTensors, the norm $ \sqrt{Re^2 + Im^2} $ is used to determine the clip.
If Tin is integer type, it will automatically be promoted to Type.Double.
For symmetric UniTensors, only the elements in the blocks are inverted.
Compared to Inv_(double clip), this function does not modify the input UniTensor but returns a new UniTensor.
Inv_(double clip)

briefdescription.para: Element-wise (pseudo-)inverse, inplacely.

detaileddescription.para:
This function performs an element-wise inverse with clip. If $ |T_\text{in}[i]| \le \mathrm{clip} $, then the new element is set to zero. \[ T_\text{in}[i] \rightarrow \left\{ \begin{array}{ll} 1/(T_\text{in}[i]) & \mathrm{if} \ |T_\text{in}[i]| > \mathrm{clip} \\ 0 & \mathrm{otherwise} \end{array} \right. \]
clip
elmements with absolute value <= clip are set to zero, corresponding to the pseudo-inverse; default: -1 (no clipping)
UniTensor
For complex type UniTensors, the norm $ \sqrt{Re^2 + Im^2} $ is used to determine the clip.
If Tin is integer type, it will automatically be promoted to Type.Double.
For symmetric UniTensors, only the elements in the blocks are inverted.
Compared to UniTensor Inv(const UniTensor &Tin, double clip), this is an inplace function, which modifies the input UniTensor.
Inv(double clip) const

briefdescription.para: Take the transpose of the UniTensor.

detaileddescription.para:
This function takes the transpose of a UniTensor: 1) The order of the indices is inverted. 2) Incoming legs become outgoing ones, and vice versa. 3) The rowrank is set to rank - old rowrank, such that left indices become right indices and vice versa.
UniTensor
This function does not only exchange left- and right indices, but inverts the order of all indices.
Compared to Transpose_(), this function will return new UniTensor object.
Transpose_()

briefdescription.para: Take the conjugate transpose to the UniTensor.

simplesect.para: This function inverts the order of all indices.
Compared to Dagger_(), this function will create a new UniTensor object.
For fermionic UniTensors, the index order is reversed without sign flips.
Dagger_(), Transpose()

briefdescription.para: Take the conjugate transpose to the UniTensor, inplacely.

simplesect.para: UniTensor&
This function inverts the order of all indices.
Compared to Dagger(), this is an inplace function.
For fermionic UniTensors, the index order is reversed without sign flips.

briefdescription.para: Set the UniTensor as a tagged UniTensor, in-place.

detaileddescription.para:
This function will set the untagged UniTensor(all of the Bonds are BondType::BD_REG) ) to tagged UniTensor (all of the Bonds are BondType::BD_BRA or BondType::BD_KET). And it will change the UniTensor to bra-ket form. See is_braket_form() for more information.
is_braket_form()

xrefdescription.para: This function is deprecated. Please use tag_() instead.

briefdescription.para: Take the power p of all elements.

parameterdescription.para: the power to take

simplesect.para: If this is a real UniTensor containing negative elements, then p must be an integer.
For symmetric UniTensors, only the elements in the blocks are inverted.
If p < 0 an inverse has to be taken. If elements could be zero (or close to it), use Inv(double clip) const for a pseudo-inverse instead.

For fermionic UniTensors, the sign structure of the blocks remains unchanged. This way, permute commutes with Pow. This can lead to unexpected behavior since T.Pow(2.0) can differ by signs from T * T.
Compared UniTensor &Pow_(const double &p), this function does not modify the input UniTensor but returns a new UniTensor.
UniTensor &Pow_(const double &p)

briefdescription.para: Take the power p of all elements, inplacely.

parameterdescription.para: the power to take

simplesect.para: If this is a real UniTensor containing negative elements, then p must be an integer.
For symmetric UniTensors, only the elements in the blocks are inverted.
If p < 0 an inverse has to be taken. If elements could be zero (or close to it), use Inv_(double clip) for a pseudo-inverse instead.

For fermionic UniTensors, the sign structure of the blocks remains unchanged. This way, permute commutes with Pow_. This can lead to unexpected behavior since T.Pow_(2.0) can differ by signs from T * T.
Compared UniTensor Pow(const double &p) const, this is an inplace function, which modifies the input UniTensor.
UniTensor &Pow(const double &p) const

briefdescription.para: Copy data from a UniTensor of different type, in-place.

detaileddescription.para:
For example, if uTB is a BlockUniTensor and uTD is a DenseUniTensor of the same shape, then uTD.convert_from_(uTB) makes uTD a sparse copy of uTB.
rhs
the UniTensor to copy from
force
if true, the compatibility of the input and output tensor structure is not checked strictly; elements that do not exist in the output tensor are ignored in the input tensor, even if they are not zero or small.
tol
applies if a denser UniTensor is converted to a sparser tensor structure; if an element in the output tensor does not exist, the absolute value of the element in the input tensor has to be <= tol. Otherwise an error is thrown. This is overwritten by force = true.
[UniTensor] a reference to this UniTensor

xrefdescription.para: This function is deprecated. Please use convert_from_(const UniTensor &rhs, bool force, cytnx_double tol) instead.

memberdef.name: arange

briefdescription.para: Create a rank-1 UniTensor with incremental elements in the range [start,end) with given step-size step between elements.

detaileddescription.para:
The elements are start, start + step, start + 2 * step, ... The last element is the largest one that is smaller than end.
start
start value of the range.
end
end value of the range (exclusive).
step
step-size between subsequent elements in the range.
in_labels
the label of the UniTensor.
dtype
the data type of the UniTensor, see cytnx::Type for more information.
device
the device type of the UniTensor, see cytnx::Device for more information.
name
the name of the UniTensor.
[UniTensor]
arange(const cytnx_double &start, const cytnx_double &end, const cytnx_double &step, const unsigned int &dtype, const int &device)

start

end

Nelem
the number of elements.
endpoint
if endpoint is true, the end of linspace is end, else the end of linspace is end - step.
in_labels
the labels of the UniTensor.
dtype

device
the device type of the UniTensor, see cytnx::Device for more information.
name
the name of the UniTensor.
